patient.query: Honestly, it feels like if I’m not perfect, it means I’m failing them in some way. Like, what if they don’t feel supported or loved because I mess up? I guess I worry that they’ll see my flaws and think it’s okay to give up when things get hard. It’s just really scary to think about that.

It's understandable that those thoughts would feel scary. patient.query: Yeah, I guess it comes from my own upbringing. I had a lot of pressure to succeed in everything, and there was this idea that mistakes were failures, not learning opportunities. It’s like, if I wasn’t perfect, I didn’t deserve praise or love. I just don’t want to pass that on to my kids. But at the same time, I’m terrified of failing them. It just feels like a never-ending cycle.
